questions.text: Who was your very first crush — how old were you and what made them stand out to you?
What was the silliest, most awkward or innocent thing you ever did because you liked someone?
Did you have a favourite love song, movie scene, book couple or TV romance when you were a teenager?
What was your idea of a perfect date when you were 13–16 years old?
Did you ever pass notes, send secret messages or use coded ways to talk to someone you liked?
What was the most memorable school dance, party or group hangout where you felt butterflies?
Who was your first real boyfriend, girlfriend or partner — how did you meet and what was the first date like?
What was one sweet, thoughtful or romantic thing someone did for you early in a relationship?
Did you ever have your heart broken for the first time — what happened and how did you cope?
Was there an early relationship (even a short one) that made you feel really seen or understood?
Looking back at your early romantic experiences, what did you learn about what you actually wanted in a partner?
Was there a time you stayed in a relationship longer than you should have — and why?
Which early romantic experience (good or painful) shaped how you approach love today the most?
What is one thing you wish you had known about relationships when you were first falling in love?
Was there a moment in an early relationship when you felt truly vulnerable — and how did it go?
What part of your early romantic life do you now realise was more about discovering yourself than about the other person?
Is there a past love or crush you now wish you had treated differently — or that you wish had treated you differently?
What do you hope your own children or future generations understand about love from your experiences?
Looking back, what one romantic memory still makes you smile, laugh or feel bittersweet?
Was there an early relationship that quietly changed how you see trust, commitment or heartbreak?
What lesson from your early romances turned out to be surprisingly important later in life?
If you could go back and give your teenage self one piece of advice about love or relationships, what would it be?
What do you hope people who knew you during your early romantic years would remember about how you loved?
Is there an early love story from your life that you now see as a quiet turning point in your path?
What do you want your future loved ones (children, grandchildren) to know about how you experienced love?
If your younger self in love could see who you are today, what do you think they would feel about your journey?
